The '''66th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], were held on 10 February 2013 at the [[Royal Opera House]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2012 in film|2012]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/date-announced-for-the-2013-orange-british-academy-film-awards |title=Date Announced for the 2013 Orange British Academy Film Awards |date=21 February 2012 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |access-date=21 February 2012}}</ref><ref name="Winners">{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-20955363 |title=Bafta Film Awards 2013: The winners |date=10 February 2013 |publisher=[[BBC]] |access-date=10 February 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/filmblog/2013/feb/10/baftas-2013-film-ceremony-live |title=Baftas 2013 – as it happened |last=Brooks |first=Xan |date=11 February 2013 |work=[[The Guardian]]|location=London |access-date=11 February 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/film/baftas/9861890/Baftas-2013-Argo-is-a-worthy-winner.html |title=Baftas 2013: Argo is a worthy winner |last=Collin |first=Robbie |date=11 February 2013 |work=[[The Daily Telegraph]]|location=London |access-date=11 February 2013}}</ref> The nominations were announced on 9 January 2013.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2013/jan/09/baftas-2013-nominations-list |title=Baftas 2013: full list of nominations |date=9 January 2013 |work=[[The Guardian]]|location=London |access-date=9 January 2013}}</ref> Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2012.

[[Stephen Fry]] hosted the ceremony, where ''[[Argo (2012 film)|Argo]]'' won Best Film and Best Director for [[Ben Affleck]].<ref name="Winners" /> [[Daniel Day-Lewis]] won Best Actor for ''[[Lincoln (film)|Lincoln]]'' and [[Emmanuelle Riva]] won Best Actress for ''[[Amour (2012 film)|Amour]]''.<ref name="Winners" /> [[Christoph Waltz]] won Best Supporting Actor for ''[[Django Unchained]]'' and [[Anne Hathaway]] won Best Supporting Actress for ''[[Les Misérables (2012 film)|Les Misérables]]''.<ref name="Winners" /> ''[[Skyfall]]'', directed by [[Sam Mendes]], was voted Outstanding British Film of 2012.<ref name="Winners" /> Sir [[Alan Parker]] received the [[BAFTA Fellowship]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/sir-alan-parker-to-be-honoured-with-bafta-fellowship |title=Sir Alan Parker to be Honoured With BAFTA Fellowship |date=23 January 2013 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |access-date=23 January 2013}}</ref> and [[Tessa Ross]] garnered the [[BAFTA Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema Award]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/tessa-ross-to-receive-bafta-award-for-outstanding-british-contribution |title=Tessa Ross to Receive BAFTA Award For Outstanding British Contribution To Cinema |date=29 January 2013 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]] |access-date=29 January 2013}}</ref>
